name: LAMMPS
description: Large-scale Atomic/Molecular Massively Parallel Simulator — an open-source molecular dynamics code for simulating the motion of atoms and molecules.
whyUseIt: LAMMPS simulates systems of atoms interacting via a wide range of potentials — metals, polymers, biological molecules, granular materials. It runs efficiently on everything from a laptop to the world's largest supercomputers. Essential for condensed matter and materials physics.
fieldPosition: Condensed matter, materials science, biophysics — atomistic and molecular simulation
physicistWorkflow: Define the system geometry and interatomic potential, set temperature and pressure, run the simulation, and analyse trajectories.
supervisorExpects: Ability to set up, run, and analyse molecular dynamics simulations with appropriate potentials and ensemble settings.
computationalImpact: Enables the study of material properties from first principles of atomic interactions — bridging quantum mechanics and macroscopic behaviour.

vocabulary.definition: The mathematical function describing the interaction between atoms
An algorithm that maintains constant temperature during the simulation

beginnerMistakes: Using an inappropriate interatomic potential for the material being studied
Not equilibrating the system before collecting data
